sections.source_quote: In this Conduct Standard, "the Act" means the Financial Sector Regulation Act, 2017 ()

sections.heading: Application and general obligations

requirements.text: A bank must conduct Its business in a manner that prioritises the fair treatment of financial customers.

sections.source_quote: A bank must conduct Its business in a manner that prioritises the fair treatment of financial customers.

sections.heading: Culture and governance
sections.summary: Banks must maintain integrity, honesty, and transparency in business practices and ensure fair treatment of financial customers through governance arrangements.
sections.dtia_summary: Banks must implement governance structures to ensure fair customer treatment and compliance with conduct standards through oversight mechanisms.

requirements.text: A bank must at all times conduct its business with integrity
A bank must act honestly, fairly, with due skill, care and diligence, and in a manner that does not bring the financial sector into disrepute
A bank must organise and manage its affairs responsibly and efficiently
A bank must avoid or, where avoidance is not possible, manage and mitigate actual or potential conflicts of interest
A bank must deal with the Authority in an open and cooperative manner
A bank must conduct its business transparently and with due regard to the Information needs of its financial customers

A bank must document, adopt, implement and monitor the effectiveness of appropriate governance arrangements that are reasonably necessary to ensure adherence to the requirements in section 2(4) and 3(1)
The governance arrangements must be proportionate to the nature, scale and complexity of the bank's activities and business model
The governance arrangements must describe how the bank will comply with this section through appropriate policies and procedures
The governance arrangements must address roles and responsibilities of the governing body and key persons
The governance arrangements must address remuneration, compensation and incentive practices
The governance arrangements must address conflicts of interest
The governance arrangements must address disclosure of interests in related and inter-related parties
The governance arrangements must address record keeping
The governance arrangements must address communication with the Authority
The governance arrangements must address decision making and management procedures, including delegation of authority arrangements
The governance arrangements must address risk management, compliance and internal control procedures, including segregation of duties and functions
The governance arrangements must address security, integrity, privacy and confidentiality of information

The governance arrangements must provide for regular risk-based monitoring and evaluation of the adequacy and effectiveness of the bank's systems, processes, procedures and internal control mechanisms
The monitoring and evaluation must consider systems, processes, procedures and internal control mechanisms to ensure compliance with this Conduct Standard and other applicable legislation

The monitoring and evaluation must consider systems, processes, procedures and internal control mechanisms to ensure the effectiveness of the bank's practices in relation to fair treatment of financial customers

The governing body of a bank is accountable for the approval, establishment, embedment, ongoing review of, and continued compliance with the bank's governance arrangements to reasonably ensure fair treatment of financial customers

sections.source_quote: A bank must at all times conduct Its business with integrity; act honestly, fairly, with due skill, care and diligence, and in a manner that does not bring the financial sector into disrepute; organise and manage its affairs responsibly and efficiently; avoid or, where avoidance is not possible, manage and mitigate actual or potential conflicts of interest; deal with the Authority in an open and cooperative manner; and conduct Its business transparently and with due regard to the Information needs of its financial customers.

sections.summary: Banks must disclose reasons for refusal, withdrawal, termination or closure of financial products/services to customers.
sections.dtia_summary: Banks must disclose reasons for refusal, withdrawal, termination or closure of financial products/services to customers.

requirements.text: When taking actions referred to in subsection (1), a bank must disclose reasons for refusal, withdrawal, termination or closure to the financial customer.
A bank may take actions without providing prior notice or reasons if compelled by law, has reasonable suspicion of illegal use, or has made necessary reports to authority.

sections.source_quote: Subject to subsection (4), a bank must, when it takes any of the actions referred to in subsection (1), disclose to the financial customer the reasons for the refusal, withdrawal, termination or closure.
